reasons: Lukas becomes more threatening when he transforms and gains stronger brawling stats, so Karrie's tank-shred profile is valuable.
Karrie can punish Lukas if she maintains distance and avoids being locked by his crowd-control chain.

proof.summary: Lukas wants to survive long brawls with enhanced stats and HP recovery, while Karrie can pressure durable fighters with repeated true-damage output.
worksBestWhen: Karrie has peel and space to keep attacking.
Lukas has already used dash or control tools.
Karrie avoids fighting in melee range.
failureCases: Lukas reaches Karrie before she can kite.
Karrie fights alone without frontline protection.
Lukas's team forces Karrie to stop attacking before marks convert.

reasons: Lukas gains HP recovery when his enhanced combo hits heroes, so anti-heal reduces his extended-fight reliability.
Baxia can stand in the frontline and reduce the value of Lukas's sustain pattern.

proof.summary: Lukas wants to recover HP while brawling, while Baxia reduces healing received and makes extended trades less favorable for him.
worksBestWhen: Baxia stays near Lukas during the brawl.
The team focuses Lukas while his recovery is reduced.
Baxia prevents Lukas from freely reaching the backline.
failureCases: Lukas ignores Baxia and dives the backline.
Baxia cannot keep contact with Lukas.
Lukas disengages until the anti-heal pressure is lower.

reasons: Alpha can contest Lukas in extended fights because Alpha has repeated true-damage output and sustain.
Lukas gains more value when fights become long brawls, so Alpha's repeated Beta-assisted damage gives a direct damage-profile answer into that pattern.

proof.summary: Lukas wants to stay in extended melee fights with HP recovery and Sacred Beast form pressure, while Alpha can keep dealing true damage through repeated Beta-assisted skill rotations.
worksBestWhen: Alpha fights Lukas before Lukas fully controls the teamfight.
Alpha lands repeated skills instead of only trading basic attacks.
Anti-heal or team focus is available to reduce Lukas's recovery.
failureCases: Lukas enters Sacred Beast form with strong team backup.
Alpha misses skills and loses the true-damage cycle.
Lukas uses mobility to bypass Alpha and reach the backline.

reasons: Kaja can lock Lukas during his committed dive and drag him away from the backline.
Suppression is valuable because Lukas's brawling strength requires uptime.

proof.summary: Lukas wants to transform, dash in, and keep fighting, while Kaja can suppress him and deny that continuous uptime.
worksBestWhen: Kaja waits for Lukas to commit to a target.
The team focuses Lukas during suppression.
Kaja avoids using suppression before Lukas's real engage.
failureCases: Kaja uses ultimate before Lukas transforms or commits.
Lukas's team counter-engages during Kaja's pick attempt.
Kaja cannot reach Lukas through frontline pressure.

reasons: Valentina can copy Lukas's ultimate state in a way that is specifically documented for Lukas, including current ultimate availability and Hero's Resolve.
This can turn Lukas's own teamfight transformation value into a mirrored transformation threat when Valentina's team can use the copied form.

proof.summary: Lukas wants to be the unique Sacred Beast form threat in teamfights, while Valentina can copy his ultimate availability and Resolve state to create a mirrored transformation threat for her own team.
worksBestWhen: Lukas has Unleash the Beast available or enough Resolve value when copied.
Valentina's team can use the copied form to contest the same teamfight timing.
Valentina does not copy too early before the fight starts.
failureCases: Valentina copies Lukas when his ultimate state has low value.
Valentina is bursted before using the copied ultimate.
The team lacks follow-up and the copied form only becomes temporary value.
